_comment: Projects in this file are clone-able anonymously and are run by the manual CI job `pages:regression`.
Each entry has to be a Rell project with valid chromia.yml
name             — workdir directory + report row label (required)
ref              — branch / tag / commit; omit for repo's default branch
rellPath         — directory containing chromia.yml, relative to repo root (default: ".")
commands         — list of chr invocations to run sequentially; non-zero exit short-circuits.
when chromia.yml has no `test:` block (chr exits with "No tests to run"),
expectedFailure  — known-bad against verified Rell builds
notes            — free-form, rendered in the report

projects.name: ft4-lib
projects.notes: FT4 token-fungibles library. Default pipeline; tests run against `ft4_rell_test` and `ft4_rell_auth` chains.

projects.name: directory-chain
projects.notes: Chromia directory service blockchain. Default pipeline; tests live under `economy_chain.test`.

projects.name: filechain
projects.notes: FileHub on-chain storage blockchain. No `test:` block — `chr test` skipped.

projects.name: filehub
projects.notes: FileHub gateway. Default pipeline; top-level `tests` module. `chr test` fails to compile against the current ft4 dev branch (`Unknown name: 'ft_auth_operation_for'` — upstream ft4 API rename that filehub hasn't picked up). Marked expectedFailure until the rename lands here.

projects.name: postchain-eif
projects.notes: Ethereum Interoperability Framework. chromia.yml under postchain-eif-rell/rell/. `test.failOnError: false` upstream keeps individual failures from aborting the suite.

projects.name: auth-server-ft4
projects.notes: FT4-aware auth server. No `test:` block — `chr test` skipped.

projects.name: ft3-lib
projects.notes: Predecessor of FT4. Historical/FT3-era; install+build pass but `chr test` hits the same downstream Rell issues as the FT4-tracking projects. Left listed to surface future regressions; not maintained forward.

projects.name: lib-pagination-utils
projects.notes: Pagination helper. chromia.yml's `module: core` points at a non-existent module (real modules at src/core/pagination/), so `chr build` has never worked. Consumed as a library; install-only.

projects.name: originals-governance
projects.notes: Originals governance dapp. No `test:` block. (Earlier runs needed an ssh→https patch for the lib-pagination-utils registry; upstream now ships the https URL, so no patch is required.)
